seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with Pydantic AI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with Pydantic AI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Pydantic AI enables building robust, type-safe AI agents for workflow automation, handling multi-step tasks with structured outputs and tool integrations. Guardrails are essential to catch validation failures, ensure action correctness, and prevent costly errors in production workflows involving API calls and human approvals. Vex integrates seamlessly as a result validator to verify agent outputs against business rules and rollback unsafe actions.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with Pydantic AI
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on an orchestrator agent that decomposes complex workflows into sequential steps executed by specialized sub-agents. Each step involves tool calls to external APIs (CRM, payment systems), conditional branching based on structured outputs, and human-in-the-loop approval gates. Pydantic models enforce output schemas while Vex validators check business invariants like idempotency and permissions.

Data flows from user requests through the orchestrator to tool agents, with intermediate results persisted in a workflow state store. Rollback mechanisms use transaction logs to reverse API actions on validation failures. Streaming responses are validated incrementally to catch mid-generation errors early.

This setup supports medium-risk workflows like order processing, where action correctness and permission validation prevent financial losses.

risks.name: Result validator rejecting valid but unexpected model outputs
risks.description: Pydantic models reject semantically correct but structurally unexpected responses, halting workflows
risks.mitigation: Vex result validators with flexible schema matching and fallback parsing

risks.name: Tool retry logic masking persistent underlying errors
risks.description: Retries succeed on transient errors but fail idempotency checks on repeated API calls
risks.mitigation: Vex idempotency verification before retry with transaction rollback

risks.name: Dependency injection context not available in nested tool calls
risks.description: Sub-agents lose workflow context (user permissions, transaction IDs) during nested execution
risks.mitigation: Vex context propagation validator ensuring all tools receive complete state

risks.name: Streaming structured responses failing mid-generation validation
risks.description: Partial streaming outputs fail Pydantic validation, corrupting workflow state
risks.mitigation: Vex incremental streaming validators with graceful degradation

implementation.title: Basic Workflow Orchestrator with Pydantic AI
implementation.description: Define a multi-step order processing agent with structured tool outputs

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop Approval Gate
implementation.description: Implement conditional approval step with external review integration

implementation.title: Idempotent API Tool with Transaction Context
implementation.description: Ensure safe retries with transaction IDs and rollback capability

implementation.title: Structured Streaming Workflow Results
implementation.description: Handle streaming outputs with incremental validation
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent
from pydantic import BaseModel

class StreamingWorkflowResult(BaseModel):
    steps: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    final_status: str

class StreamingOrchestrator(Agent):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__(
            model='gpt-4o',
            result_model=StreamingWorkflowResult,
            stream=True,
            instructions='Stream workflow progress step-by-step'
        )

    async def execute_streaming(self, task: str):
        async for chunk in self.run(task):
            # Incremental validation on each chunk
            partial_result = StreamingWorkflowResult.model_validate(chunk)
            yield partial_result

orchestrator = StreamingOrchestrator()
# Usage: async for step in orchestrator.execute_streaming('process order 123')

vexIntegration.title: Context Propagation Validator for Nested Calls
vexIntegration.description: Ensure dependency injection context flows through nested tool execution
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexValidator

context_validator = VexValidator(
    name="nested-context-propagation",
    rules=[
        {
            "type": "context_check",
            "required": ["user_id", "tenant_id", "transaction_id"],
            "condition": "all(key in ctx for key in self.required)",
            "error_message": "Missing workflow context in nested call"
        },
        {
            "type": "permission_inheritance",
            "condition": "ctx.permissions == parent_ctx.permissions",
            "action": "propagate_context"
        }
    ],
    context_providers=[
        "auth.user",
        "workflow.transaction",
        "tenant.config"
    ]
)

# Global registration
Agent.default_validators.append(context_validator)

cta.heading: Secure Your Pydantic AI Workflow Automation
